question_title: What will be the value of "10-Year Treasury Constant Maturity Minus Federal Funds Rate" on 2026-03-12?
summary: The T10YFF spread (10-Year Treasury Constant Maturity minus Federal Funds Rate) is currently 0.38 (Feb 26). The EFFR is fixed at 3.64 with no Fed rate change expected before March 12, so all movement comes from the 10-year Treasury yield. The spread has declined from ~0.65 in early February to 0.38, though it has stabilized over the last 4 trading days. Monte Carlo simulation (100k paths, 10 trading days, ~3.7 bps/day normal vol with augmented event-day volatility) centers the distribution at ~0.36, reflecting a mild continuation of the downward drift tempered by recent stabilization. Key event risk comes from the jobs report (Mar 6), CPI (Mar 11), and PPI (Mar 12), which add substantial volatility and make the distribution wider than pure random-walk estimates.

factors.description: Current level is 0.38 as of Feb 26. EFFR is constant at 3.64 with no rate change expected before resolution, so all movement comes from 10-year Treasury yield changes.

Mild downward trend over last 30 trading days: spread declined from ~0.65 to 0.38, daily drift ~-0.004. However last 4 days show stabilization (0.38-0.41), suggesting trend is decelerating. Used moderated drift of -0.002/day in simulation.

Major event risk: Jobs report (Mar 6), CPI (Mar 11), PPI (Mar 12) all before/on resolution date. Historical daily changes include jumps of 0.26-0.28 on event days. These events increase distribution width substantially.

Fed funds futures imply slight easing expectations (3.60 in May vs 3.64 now), but no cut before March 12. 10yr yield at 4.02 is near recent lows. Data surprises in either direction could move the spread significantly.

reasoning:
**Forecast: T10YFF on 2026-03-12**

| Percentile | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| P10 | 0.19 |
| P20 | 0.25 |
| P40 | 0.32 |
| P60 | 0.39 |
| P80 | 0.47 |
| P90 | 0.53 |

**Key reasoning:**
- Current value: 0.38 (Feb 26). EFFR locked at 3.64 — all movement comes from the 10-year yield
- Mild negative drift (-0.002/day) reflecting the slowing downtrend from 0.65 → 0.38 over the past month
- Three major data releases before resolution — NFP (Mar 6), CPI (Mar 11), PPI (Mar 12) — significantly widen the distribution
- Monte Carlo simulation (100k paths, event-augmented volatility) centers at ~0.36 with median slightly below the current 0.38
sources_consulted: [(Metaculus) What will be the value of "10-Year Treasury Constant Maturity Minus Federal Funds Rate" on 2026-03-12?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/42385)
